crop_context: maize fields considered for AMF inoculation
microbiome_indicator: soil microbial biomass and pathogen-related community signatures
indicator_direction: predictive mixture of positive and negative indicators
support_signal: higher predicted AMF growth response in specific soil-microbiome states
risk_signal: poor microbiome contexts predict lower response
generalizability_note: likely useful but context-dependent rather than universal
notes: Key source for response-sensitive state logic rather than simple health labels.

crop_context: agricultural soils across management depth profiles
management_context: fertility source tillage and cover crop management
microbiome_indicator: fungal modules and microbial assembly features
support_signal: more supportive assembly patterns under some fertility regimes
risk_signal: interaction structure can be weakened under some management conditions
generalizability_note: depth and management effects limit universal interpretation
notes: Useful for linking soil state to management rather than only crop type.

crop_context: crop systems under sustainable soil management
microbiome_indicator: microbiome diversity and plant-defense linked changes
support_signal: increased crop defense and beneficial microbiome association under supportive management
risk_signal: less supportive states under conventional pressure or weaker diversity
generalizability_note: likely system-dependent and should not be generalized without context
notes: Good support-side anchor for the classifier.

crop_context: watermelon rhizosphere under different cropping and amendment systems
support_signal: healthy-associated indicators appear in some systems
risk_signal: indicator transfer breaks under diverse management and cropping systems
generalizability_note: explicitly shows that cross-system generalization is limited
notes: Essential for preventing overclaiming in the classifier.

crop_context: multiple crops under environmental stress and agronomic practices
microbiome_indicator: beneficial genera and stress-linked microbiome shifts
support_signal: some taxa and interactions align with better adaptation
risk_signal: persistent stress and harmful practices can degrade support functions
generalizability_note: review-level synthesis and not enough for universal deployment alone
notes: Useful for defining the stressed-state side of the vocabulary.

crop_context: arable farming systems under diverse management
management_context: soil testing and routine indicator context
microbiome_indicator: microbial indicators used in soil-health testing
indicator_direction: indicator prioritization rather than binary direction
support_signal: helps prioritize which indicators may deserve routine attention
risk_signal: shows not all indicators are equally sensitive or useful
generalizability_note: sensitivity depends on management practice and soil context
notes: Useful for deciding which indicators belong in a practical field-facing panel.
